revision.text:
{{infobox ancient site | name = Kobe Site | native_name =小部遺跡 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kobe Site | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|33|25|N|131|20|16|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Usa, Ōita]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Settlement trace | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th-6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|33|25|N|131|20|16|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kobe Site'''|小部遺跡|}} is a complex [[archaeological site]] with the traces of a [[Kofun period]] settlement, located in the Araki neighborhood of the city of [[Usa, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2021.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/517604|title=小部遺跡|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kobe Site is located at the east end of a low terrace overlooking the west basin of the Tsukan River, and the Kurokuro River. It contains various remains from the late [[Yayoi period]] to the [[Kamakura period]]; however, the traces of a ring-moated settlement with pottery from the early [[Kofun period]] is particularly important. As a result of ten [[archaeological excavation]]s, it is estimated that the plan of the settlement was rectangular or irregularly circular with rounded corners, approximately 120 meters on each side. On the west side, there are the traces of at least two large large pillared buildings measuring 10 to 15 meters on each side, which are of a later date than the surrounding moats. The state of the buildings inside the moat is unknown because a full-scale survey has not been conducted, but as it is contemporary with the Akatsuka Kofun, the oldest tumulus in the nearby [[Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]], it is likely to have been a settlement occupied by the local rulers whose tombs are the [[zenpokoenfun|tumuli]] in that ''kofun'' cluster.
Many pieces of pottery from the [[Kansai region]] and [[Kingdom of Kibi|Kibi region]] have been excavated from the ruins, and it is believed that it was one of the trading centers in the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/sougo/soshiki/18/shakaikyoiku/3/bunkazai/12565.html Usa City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

The state of the buildings inside the moat is unknown because a full-scale survey has not been conducted, but as it is contemporary with the Akatsuka Kofun, the oldest tumulus in the nearby [[Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]], it is likely to have been a settlement occupied by the local rulers whose tombs are the [[zenpokoenfun|tumuli]] in that ''kofun'' cluster.
Many pieces of pottery from the [[Kansai region]] and [[Kingdom of Kibi|Kibi region]] have been excavated from the ruins, and it is believed that it was one of the trading centers in the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]

{{nihongo|'''Kobe Site'''|小部遺跡|}} is a complex [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] with the traces of a [[Kofun period]] settlement, located in the Araki neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2021.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/517604|title=小部遺跡|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kobe Site is located at the east end of a low terrace overlooking the west basin of the Tsukan River, and the Kurokuro River. It contains various remains from the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]; however, the traces of a ring-moated settlement with pottery from the early [[Kofun period]] is particularly important. As a result of ten [[archaeological excavation]]s, it is estimated that the plan of the settlement was rectangular or irregularly circular with rounded corners, approximately 120 meters on each side. On the west side, there are the traces of at least two large large pillared buildings measuring 10 to 15 meters on each side, which are of a later date than the surrounding moats. The state of the buildings inside the moat is unknown because a full-scale survey has not been conducted, but as it is contemporary with the Akatsuka Kofun, the oldest tumulus in the nearby [[Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]], it is likely to have been a settlement occupied by the local rulers whose tombs are the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|tumuli]] in that ''kofun'' cluster.
Many pieces of pottery from the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] and [[Kingdom of Kibi|Kibi region]] have been excavated from the ruins, and it is believed that it was one of the trading centers in the [[Seto Inland Sea]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kobe Site]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kobe Site]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kobe Site]] {{infobox ancient site | name = Kobe Site | native_name =小部遺跡 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kobe Site | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|33|25|N|131|20|16|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Settlement trace | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th-6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|33|25|N|131|20|16|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kobe Site'''|小部遺跡|}} is a complex [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] with the traces of a [[Kofun period]] settlement, located in the Araki neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Usa, Ōita|Usa, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2021.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/517604|title=小部遺跡|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kobe Site is located at the east end of a low terrace overlooking the west basin of the Tsukan River, and the Kurokuro River. It contains various remains from the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]; however, the traces of a ring-moated settlement with pottery from the early [[Kofun period]] is particularly important. As a result of ten [[archaeological excavation]]s, it is estimated that the plan of the settlement was rectangular or irregularly circular with rounded corners, approximately 120 meters on each side. On the west side, there are the traces of at least two large large pillared buildings measuring 10 to 15 meters on each side, which are of a later date than the surrounding moats. The state of the buildings inside the moat is unknown because a full-scale survey has not been conducted, but as it is contemporary with the Akatsuka Kofun, the oldest tumulus in the nearby [[Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]], it is likely to have been a settlement occupied by the local rulers whose tombs are the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|tumuli]] in that ''kofun'' cluster.
Many pieces of pottery from the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] and [[Kingdom of Kibi|Kibi region]] have been excavated from the ruins, and it is believed that it was one of the trading centers in the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/sougo/soshiki/18/shakaikyoiku/3/bunkazai/12565.html Usa City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

Many pieces of pottery from the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] and [[Kingdom of Kibi|Kibi region]] have been excavated from the ruins, and it is believed that it was one of the trading centers in the [[Seto Inland Sea]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kobe Site]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kobe Site]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kobe Site]] {{infobox ancient site | name = Kobe Site | native_name =小部遺跡 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kobe Site | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|33|25|N|131|20|16|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa, Ōita}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Settlement trace | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th-6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|33|25|N|131|20|16|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kobe Site'''|小部遺跡|}} is a complex {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site}} with the traces of a [[Kofun period]] settlement, located in the Araki neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|WD=Q114147|lt=Usa, Ōita}}, on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2021.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/517604|title=小部遺跡|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kobe Site is located at the east end of a low terrace overlooking the west basin of the Tsukan River, and the Kurokuro River. It contains various remains from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} to the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}; however, the traces of a ring-moated settlement with pottery from the early [[Kofun period]] is particularly important. As a result of ten [[archaeological excavation]]s, it is estimated that the plan of the settlement was rectangular or irregularly circular with rounded corners, approximately 120 meters on each side. On the west side, there are the traces of at least two large large pillared buildings measuring 10 to 15 meters on each side, which are of a later date than the surrounding moats. The state of the buildings inside the moat is unknown because a full-scale survey has not been conducted, but as it is contemporary with the Akatsuka Kofun, the oldest tumulus in the nearby [[Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]], it is likely to have been a settlement occupied by the local rulers whose tombs are the {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=tumuli}} in that ''kofun'' cluster.
Many pieces of pottery from the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} and [[Kingdom of Kibi|Kibi region]] have been excavated from the ruins, and it is believed that it was one of the trading centers in the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|WD=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/sougo/soshiki/18/shakaikyoiku/3/bunkazai/12565.html Usa City home page]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

{{nihongo|'''Kobe Site'''|小部遺跡|}} is a complex {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|qid=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} with the traces of a [[Kofun period]] settlement, located in the Araki neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Usa, Ōita|en|Usa, Ōita|qid=Q114147|lt=Usa, Ōita|1=Usa, Ōita}}, on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2021.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/517604|title=小部遺跡|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kobe Site is located at the east end of a low terrace overlooking the west basin of the Tsukan River, and the Kurokuro River. It contains various remains from the late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} to the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}; however, the traces of a ring-moated settlement with pottery from the early [[Kofun period]] is particularly important. As a result of ten [[archaeological excavation]]s, it is estimated that the plan of the settlement was rectangular or irregularly circular with rounded corners, approximately 120 meters on each side. On the west side, there are the traces of at least two large large pillared buildings measuring 10 to 15 meters on each side, which are of a later date than the surrounding moats. The state of the buildings inside the moat is unknown because a full-scale survey has not been conducted, but as it is contemporary with the Akatsuka Kofun, the oldest tumulus in the nearby [[Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster]], it is likely to have been a settlement occupied by the local rulers whose tombs are the {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=tumuli|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} in that ''kofun'' cluster.
Many pieces of pottery from the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} and [[Kingdom of Kibi|Kibi region]] have been excavated from the ruins, and it is believed that it was one of the trading centers in the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|qid=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.usa.oita.jp/sougo/soshiki/18/shakaikyoiku/3/bunkazai/12565.html Usa City home page]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
